title: Adjective case endings
subtitle: How adjectives change with the noun through cases

hook.text: Adjectives in Russian don't just agree in gender — they change ending for every case too. When the noun changes case, its adjective changes with it. нового, новому, новым — these are all forms of новый. This lesson maps out the adjective endings across the cases you've learned, using the masculine as the base.

why.text: Adjective case endings follow a clear pattern — in fact, they're more regular than noun endings. Once you learn the masculine adjective paradigm, feminine and neuter follow predictable variations. The goal today is to see the full picture, not to memorise every form simultaneously. Recognition before production.

cards.title: Masculine adjective endings — all cases
cards.body: The full masculine adjective paradigm for a hard-stem adjective like новый. Notice: genitive and accusative (animate) share the same ending -ОГО. Dative and prepositional differ by one letter.

cards.title: Feminine adjective endings — all cases
cards.body: Feminine adjectives have a unified accusative ending (-УЮ) — different from both nominative and genitive. This is distinctive and worth noting.

cards.title: Key observation — genitive and animate accusative
cards.body: The rule you learned for animate masculine nouns (accusative = genitive) applies to adjectives too. Я вижу нового студента — both the adjective and noun are in genitive form for animate accusative. This is why genitive matters so much.

examples.meaning: I see a new student. (adj. нового + noun студента — both animate acc. = gen.)
I see a new table. (adj. новый + noun стол — inanimate: no change)

questions.prompt: What is the genitive of новый стол (new table)?
questions.explanation: новый → нового (masculine genitive). стол → стола. нового стола.

questions.prompt: What is 'I see a new book' — accusative?
questions.explanation: Feminine accusative: новая → новую, книга → книгу.

questions.prompt: What is the prepositional of новый урок?
questions.explanation: новый → новом (masculine prepositional). урок → уроке. о новом уроке.

cards.title: The Г→В shift in genitive
cards.body: In adjective genitive endings, the letter Г is pronounced like В: нового is pronounced 'novovo', своего is 'svoyevo'. This is a pronunciation rule — the spelling keeps Г but the sound is V. It applies to -ОГО/-ЕГО endings.

cards.title: Practical priority for adjective cases
cards.body: At A1 level, focus on: (1) nominative agreement — get the basic form right; (2) feminine accusative -УЮ — appears in every transitive sentence with a feminine object; (3) masculine animate accusative = genitive ending -ОГО. These three cover 80% of adjective case usage at your level.

questions.prompt: Accusative of новая машина (new car — direction: I am going to a new shop):
questions.explanation: Feminine accusative: новая → новую, машина → машину.

questions.prompt: I see an interesting student: Я вижу ___. (интересный студент)
questions.explanation: Animate masculine accusative = genitive. интересный → интересного, студент → студента.

questions.prompt: About the last lesson: о ___ уроке
With a young student: с ___ студентом
What is the feminine accusative ending for adjectives?
I study Russian: Я учу ___ язык.
questions.explanation: язык is inanimate masculine — accusative = nominative. русский язык.

rules: Feminine accusative is -УЮ — always different from nominative -АЯ
⚠ Animate masculine accusative: adjective takes -ОГО (same as genitive)
summary.nextLesson: Next: key adjective vocabulary + comparatives — bigger, better, more difficult.

questions.prompt: Accusative of новая книга (I read a new book):
I see a young student (animate): Я вижу ___.
Prepositional of интересный урок (about an interesting lesson):
The feminine accusative adjective ending is:
Instrumental of новый друг (with a new friend):
I study Russian language: Я учу ___ язык.
Genitive of -ОГО: how is Г pronounced?
